entry.title: License Update: UltrafastSecp256k1 → MIT
entry.summary:
In recent days, there has been a noticeable increase in communication regarding a specific library, with four separate threads initiated over a span of three days. This behavior suggests a high level of activity and updates surrounding the library, yet it raises concerns about the efficiency and organization of information dissemination. The creation of multiple topics for every new update on the library may not be the most effective approach to sharing news or developments.
The repeated initiation of threads could potentially lead to fragmentation of discussion and dilute the focus from the main features or updates of the library. A more consolidated form of communication might be beneficial, where updates are compiled and shared in a single thread. This would not only streamline the information flow but also enhance the readability and accessibility of updates for those interested in the library's progress. Consolidating updates into fewer, more comprehensive threads could foster more meaningful discussions and engagement from the community.
